text: Both the Income Capitalization and Sales Comparison Approaches were developed and analyzed, as they were determined to be the most applicable and reliable for estimating market value given the property’s age, occupancy, and market positioning. Greater weight was placed on the Income Approach due to the property’s use as an income-generating asset and the prevalence of investor and owner-user activity within the competitive set. The Sales Comparison Approach supported the indicated value, further substantiating the market conclusions. The Cost Approach was not developed, as it was not relevant for the subject’s market segment. The reconciled value represents the appraiser’s independent and unbiased opinion based on market evidence, property inspection, and analysis of local economic and property-specific factors appropriate to the assignment.
